No hallucinated paragraphs or sentences were found.
The URL 'data.nconemap.com' found in the output for Slide 2 matches the source document exactly. No other contact information is present in the document.

The document has 3 pages (slides). The JSON contains three main sections ('NC OneMap Update', 'Data Releases', and 'Tasks') which correspond to the three slides in the source. No pages or sections were omitted.
There are no tables in the source document; the content consists of text and lists.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source document and the output JSON. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items. Each content block from the source appears exactly once in the corresponding section of the JSON.

The heading hierarchy is correctly represented. Each slide's main title ('NC OneMap Update', 'Data Releases', 'Tasks') is used as a section title in the JSON, creating a logical flat hierarchy appropriate for a presentation format.
There are no tables present in the source document.

The reading order in the JSON follows the natural sequence of the presentation slides (Page 1 to Page 3). Within each slide, the order of headings, introductory text, and list items matches the visual flow of the original document.

The lists in the document have complex multi-level nesting (up to 3 levels on page 3). The JSON accurately captures this nesting structure, correctly identifying parent and child items based on the visual indentation and bullet types used in the source PDF.
The URL 'data.nconemap.com' present on page 2 is correctly captured as text in the JSON. No broken or fabricated links were introduced.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the multi-slide layout of the PDF into a single-column, linear web document. The visual organization of information (titles, subheadings, and lists) matches the logical flow and intent of the source slides. Information can be found in the same relative order as the original document.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; the content consists of text and bulleted lists.

The source PDF on page 3 contains three green checkmark icons representing the status (completed) of specific infrastructure tasks. In the HTML output, these meaningful icons are missing from their context within the list. Instead, a single, oversized checkmark image is rendered at the very bottom of the document, detached from the relevant data. This results in a loss of meaningful visual information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 of the PDF shows three green checkmark icons to the right of the bullet points under 'Infrastructure buildout'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows the list items without the checkmarks, and a single large green checkmark appears at the bottom of the page out of context.
checks.b_evidence_location: Tasks section, Ongoing -> Infrastructure buildout sub-list

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content duplication. Each slide's unique text and bullet points appear once in the output document. Header/navigation repetitions are excluded as per instructions.
